burden.source: WHO Global Health Observatory or DataSUS

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Systolic BP ≥140 mmHg or diastolic BP ≥90 mmHg on two or more occasions

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows JNC 8 and Brazilian Society of Cardiology guidelines: BP ≥140/90 mmHg
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Lifestyle modifications first; pharmacological therapy with ACE inhibitors, ARBs, calcium channel blockers, or diuretics per SBC guidelines
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access in rural and northern regions; low adherence due to socioeconomic barriers
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Universal coverage via SUS; nationwide screening programs like Hiperdia
